comment.body:
there is no "layman terms".. https://open.substack.com/chat/posts/398075ab-2aac-41d1-bf37-8fda43ccf9aa?target_reply_id=ec4981d8-090e-4506-a5bf-f35f178d7209&showTarget=true

liquidity and volatility are inversely proportional.
Plp are used to markets when vol is just spiking up, or only goes are 20% lets say... but not such high vol during days, weeks... low liquidity, one side is crowded, the whole system is in emergency mode. 
Plus this reduces investor confidence. 
Managers, banks, liquidity providers must juice liquidity up in order to save the market's ass... same mechanisms happened during 2008, 2022, etc. 
 They must kill the one-sided market.

comment.body:
And bcs this is a multifactoral game... i mean, Xi makes decisions, Trump makes decisions, etc. they do it independently, they react to each other, etc. We better don't get married to a certain bias. In terms of markets.
There are things that are sure.. like my "EU approaching China" call, or the isolation tendency from Trump that will lead to a multilateral wordorder, that is the most instable, etc. but you can't predict what they will need to do next, bcs most of the times even they don't know! 
This is a process that is happening.
But in terms of market effects, the timing and the effect is different

comment.body:
To wash out staglation risk, Trump decided to use the wealth effect. 
And bcs the inflation is high, market literally needs to price inflation out of the market, deflationary fears must be awakening (bcs thats how stagflation gets priced out), and this risks a recession.
Okay, no problem. JPM, Blackrock and the other CEOs assured Trump that they have every tool to bail out the economy.
Ok.
So lets price out inflation first.
